description: Did I mention "easy"? Portainer CE is intuitive and well designed. If you're new to Docker containers, Portainer CE is a great resource to help you learn. If you're an "old hat" at it, you'll wonder why you haven't been using it all along.
author: Eric M.
pros: Portainer CE very easily lets you manage stand-alone images, stacks, containers, volumes, networks, and logs. It also easily integrates with your image registries like DockerHub, AWS ECR, Azure, or even your self-hosted Gitlb.
cons: Later versions of Portainer CE no longer support external templates as a json file. Bummer. That means if you want to create your own template file, you have to write compose code for each app individually, and store the code directly in the Portainer CE application... which means it's stored in the Portainer CE data store rather than as a file.

review_title: The essential GUI for managing Docker environments and Stacks
description: A lightweight web solution was required to handle the management of our own development servers running internally in-house solutions. One such platform that caught my attention was Portainer, which is way easier to set up than Rancher for simple Docker Standalone or Swarm environments, giving immediate visibility into the health of your containers for your entire team.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Portainer has made the management of the container so easy that even without knowing all the commands in Docker, it has been an absolute pleasure to use. I find the Stacks functionality extremely useful, allowing us to deploy docker-compose files right through the GUI. And the graphical representation of the Volumes, Networks is outstanding.
cons: RBAC or granularity for multi-user teams is a little limited in the Community Edition compared to the Business Edition. Sometimes the logs viewer in the browser is a bit laggy with very verbose containers, and the UI does not always auto-refresh instantly when a container status changes externally via CLI.

description: Overall, Portainer is a great container management tool that works with Kubernets, Docker, Docker Swarm, and Azure ACI.
author: Yuko H.
pros: You can manage your containers easily. We use Portainer for Docker container management. We have many test environments that live in Docker swarm and we appreciate the easy management Portainer provides. It works with Kubernets, Docker, Docker Swarm, and Azure ACI.
cons: Because it is open source, it does not have enterprise level support services, but I didn't need it.

description: I think overall about portainer is it is a tool that makes it easy to use docker images maintain and make easy to make it also very easy, no need to code in CLI again and again, it has all features that CLI provides with GUI means graphically also it is very good so definitely suggested to use it

pros: We are using docker for a long time and we face too many issues in setup our docker file and maintaining the file, after using portainer we are very connected with docker also and portainer is given us more comfortable while using docker images and maintaining it, this is a very good option in the deployment of our software and etc. So this is all about Portainer
cons: i do not face any issue till now, it is very easy to use and has excellent processing also make your work easy

review_title: The best container management tool I've seen
description: I'm managing all my containers with Portainer both at work and at home. It's my goto must have tool for docker container management.
author: Adam D.
pros: So easy to manage, monitor, create docker containers and stacks. I'm a visual person the ability to do all of the advanced docker stuff through a web gui was a dream come true. Additionally I can just add Portainer to my dev stacks and it's always there when I'm working.
cons: You lose some features if you're not running docker in swarm mode and if you create your docker stacks outside of Portainer.

description: We use it with some client and Homelab

review_title: Portainer - to manage containers and images
description: Within 30 minutes I had the portainer environment on a simple AWS instance with 6 docker images running and was able to start / stop containers as well as shell into an existing container that is up and running.
author: Rob B.
pros: The fact that portainer runs in a container is great. No fat clients or install procedure but a couple of simple steps and presto. The interface is easy to use and simple to navigate. I use it to manage docker containers but it supports Kubernetes and others as well.
cons: As far as i have seen now (and I havent used all functionality) it looks well rounded. I am still looking for something that does not function as expected.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

cons: Limited to DOcker only, Does not support non-docker environment
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easiest way to manage Docker containers
description: Awesome tool to manage containers and see real time information of what's Docker currently doing.
author: Hector H.
pros: Easy to use works from a Raspberry Pi to a full fledged server, you can deploy images easily, make backups from a container that already has your configuration, it consumes little resources
cons: It may get buggy deploying images from time to time, but restarting the server does fix it.

description: Overall experience with the Portainer is great because it has made me more productive and with the help of Portainer, I can easily and quickly deploy containerize applications so it's best for me.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Portainer is the best way for me to manage my docker containers because I always hate to work with the terminal that's why I love Portainer because it provides good UI to work with docker containers so it's easy to use.
cons: I honestly do not dislike anything about Portainer.

review_title: Containermanagement made easy via a nice GUI
description: Installation:Installing Portainer was very simple, we just pulled the image from Docker Hub and were up and running in minutes.Day-to-day usage:We configure and deploy containers via Portainer and also monitor them this way. We have also added a private registry of Docker images that we use to quickly deploy our most commonly used containers.
author: Matthias S.
pros: Portainers graphical user interface is modern and intuitive, making it very easy to get used to. The resource monitoring for containers is also quite useful.Portainer comes with a registry of commonly used containers, which makes using it almost feel like a sort of app-store for containers.Editing dockerfiles and configuring containers via the web-interface is a lot nicer than doing so via the command-line.
cons: Since Portainer lists all Docker containers (including itself), I accidentally blew up my first Portainer installation when I changed some setting on it without thinking about it. I am aware that this is user error but a small warning when messing with the Portainer container would be nice.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Gustavo M.

cons: Pelo tempo que e forma que uso não tenho nenhum contra.
